name: Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra
name_original: Silo de Santa Amalia por La Morra — circular desde Santa Amalia
description: Jog the 5.1-mile (8.2 km) Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra route, a moderate, mostly paved circular trail with minimal elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Santa Amalia offer a variety of options through its natural surroundings and dedicated running paths. The region, located in the province of Badajoz, Spain, features both urban and semi-urban circuits, as well as access to significant natural areas. Runners can explore routes near the Guadiana Natural Trail, providing river views, and the Cornalvo Natural Park, known for its oak dehesas and historic Roman features. The terrain includes mostly paved sections and natural trails, catering to different preferences. Best jogging routes around Santa Amalia The most popular jogging route is Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the reservoir within Cornalvo Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route is mostly paved, making it suitable for a steady run through the local area. Local runners also love the El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo, a 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail leading through the Cornalvo Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging around Santa Amalia is defined by dedicated running routes, river views along the Guadiana, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy jogs to more demanding runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Amalia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Santa Amalia?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Santa Amalia, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels and preferences. These include trails through natural surroundings and specific running paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Santa Amalia?
A: The routes around Santa Amalia offer varied terrain. You'll find mostly paved sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284024478/silo-de-santa-amalia-by-la-morra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra&lt;/a&gt;, as well as natural trails, especially within areas like the Cornalvo Natural Park and along the Guadiana Natural Trail. This mix caters to both urban and more rugged running experiences.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Santa Amalia?
A: Yes, Santa Amalia offers easy running routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280334767/castillo-de-medellin-circular-por-las-vegas-altas-del-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2 km (2 miles) circular path, ideal for a gentle jog.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails in the Santa Amalia area?
A: For longer runs, consider trails that venture into the nearby natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is a challenging 18.3 km (11.4 miles) trail through Cornalvo Natural Park, offering a more demanding experience.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes available around Santa Amalia?
A: Absolutely. Many running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284024478/silo-de-santa-amalia-by-la-morra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.2 km (5.1 miles) loop, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282803562/views-of-the-castle-by-the-guadiana-river-circular-from-medellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while jogging around Santa Amalia?
A: While jogging, you can encounter various points of interest. Routes near Cornalvo Natural Park offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;. Other trails might lead you past historic features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt; or offer scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of Medellín Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails near Santa Amalia?
A: Many natural trails around Santa Amalia, especially those in less populated areas or natural parks like Cornalvo, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and check for specific local regulations or signage, particularly in protected areas, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Santa Amalia?
A: Yes, the varied terrain and range of difficulties mean there are routes suitable for families. Easy and moderate trails, often with mostly paved sections or gentle natural paths, are ideal. The shorter circular routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280334767/castillo-de-medellin-circular-por-las-vegas-altas-del-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, are great options for families looking to enjoy a run together.

Q: Is parking available near the jogging trails in Santa Amalia?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For trails within Santa Amalia itself, you can often find street parking. For routes accessing natural areas like Cornalvo Natural Park or the Guadiana Natural Trail, there are typically designated parking areas at trailheads or nearby access points. It's recommended to check specific route details or local maps for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santa Amalia?
A: The best time to go running in Santa Amalia is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable and enjoyable outdoor activity.

Q: Are there running opportunities within natural parks near Santa Amalia?
A: Yes, Santa Amalia is well-situated for accessing natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route within Cornalvo Natural Park, offering scenic views and a chance to experience the region's oak dehesas and historic Roman features. The Guadiana Natural Trail is also accessible nearby, providing diverse lengths and river views.
tips: Santa Amalia is located on the plain of the Río Guadiana but separated from it by numerous hills or "hills" that have been formed by the passage of the river over the years. Beautiful streets with white houses.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate run. Its mostly paved surfaces and minimal elevation gain (around 20 meters) make it accessible and suitable for individuals of various skill levels, including beginners looking for a comfortable jogging experience.

faqs.question: Is the Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, which contributes to its accessibility and suitability for a comfortable jogging experience. You can expect a relatively flat or gently undulating path.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific detailed descriptions for this exact route are limited, the trail is located in Santa Amalia, within the Extremadura region of Spain. This area is known for its natural landscapes, often featuring forests of chestnut, oak, and holm-oak trees, providing a pleasant backdrop for your run. You will also pass through the settlement of Santa Amalia and near the Silo of Santa Amalia.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is named after the Silo of Santa Amalia, which is a notable feature. You will also pass through the town of Santa Amalia itself.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts from Santa Amalia, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for available street parking or designated public parking areas in Santa Amalia to begin your run.

faqs.question: Is the Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, generally, paved and accessible routes like this one are suitable for dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain the trail for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra jogging route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can experience hot summers. Therefore, the spring and autumn months are generally the most pleasant for jogging, offering milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though it might be cooler.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby in Santa Amalia?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in the settlement of Santa Amalia, you should be able to find local cafes, bars, or restaurants within the town to refuel or relax after your run.
